color.quote: The image shows a soybean field with mostly green canopy and a patch of pale yellow/chlorotic plants in the right-center foreground, with no individual lesion colors clearly resolvable at this distance.
Lesions appear as small yellow-brown to reddish-brown specks and patches scattered across otherwise green leaves, with the rightmost leaf showing larger coalesced tan-brown necrotic areas.

shape.value: small irregular angular specks coalescing into larger irregular blotches
shape.quote: Individual lesions are tiny irregular flecks distributed across the leaf surface that merge into larger irregular necrotic patches on the more advanced leaf at right.

margin.quote: Lesion edges are irregular and somewhat diffuse rather than sharply defined, blending into surrounding tissue without a strong halo visible at this resolution.

texture.quote: The leaves show puckered and crinkled surfaces with necrotic dry patches and several torn holes where dead tissue has dropped out, consistent with advanced bacterial pustule damage.

color.quote: Numerous small lesions appear as dark reddish-brown to nearly black flecks surrounded by diffuse yellow chlorotic halos on a green leaf, with some larger necrotic patches that have torn out leaving holes.

shape.value: angular to irregular small spots, some coalescing
shape.quote: Lesions are mostly small, angular to irregular flecks scattered across the leaf, with several merging into larger blotches and a few that have dropped out as ragged holes.

margin.value: yellow chlorotic halo around dark necrotic center
margin.quote: Each spot has a sharply defined dark necrotic center bordered by a noticeable yellow chlorotic halo, and the larger coalesced lesions show ragged, torn edges where tissue has fallen out.

texture.quote: The lesion centers look dry and papery and several have fallen out leaving shot-hole tears; no fuzzy growth, ooze, or sporulation is visible on the leaf surface.

color.quote: Lesions are dark reddish-brown to nearly black with paler tan centers and faint yellow chlorotic halos surrounding many spots on a green leaf background.

shape.value: small angular to irregular spots, often coalescing into larger blotches with shot-holes where centers have dropped out
shape.quote: Spots are small and angular where confined by leaf veins, with several lesions coalescing into larger irregular blotches and tattered holes where necrotic tissue has fallen out.

margin.value: sharply defined dark brown border surrounded by a narrow chlorotic yellow halo, no visible water-soaking
margin.quote: Lesion margins are sharply defined by a dark brown border surrounded by a narrow chlorotic yellow halo, with no visible water-soaking at this advanced stage.

texture.quote: Affected tissue is dry and necrotic, with the centers of older lesions having dropped out to leave shot-hole and ragged tears across the leaf blade.

color.quote: The trifoliate leaves show a mottled pattern of light green to yellowish patches alternating with darker green areas, with no distinct necrotic browning.

shape.quote: Symptoms appear as diffuse, irregularly shaped chlorotic patches scattered across the leaf blade rather than discrete circular lesions.

margin.quote: The boundaries between light and dark green tissue are gradual and blurred with no defined edge or halo.

texture.quote: The leaf surface is noticeably puckered and rugose, with raised dark-green blistered areas and sunken lighter zones giving a crinkled appearance.

color.quote: Affected leaves show a patchy mosaic of light green to yellow-green areas interspersed with normal dark green tissue, with no necrotic browning visible.

shape.quote: The discolored areas appear as irregular, diffuse blotches and mottled patches scattered across the leaflets rather than as discrete circular lesions.

margin.quote: The boundaries between light and dark green areas are indistinct and gradually blend into surrounding tissue without sharply defined borders.

texture.quote: Leaf surfaces look relatively flat without obvious blistering or strong rugosity, though several leaves show small holes consistent with chewing-insect feeding alongside the mottling.

color.quote: The exposed inner stem tissue is a silvery to ashy gray, peppered with fine black microsclerotia, set against the surrounding reddish-brown outer bark.

shape.value: elongated lens-shaped lesion along the stem axis
shape.quote: The discolored area forms an elongated, oval to lens-shaped patch running lengthwise along the stem where the bark has been peeled back.

margin.quote: The boundary between the ashy gray inner tissue and the surrounding brown bark is reasonably well-defined but irregular and slightly diffuse along its edges.

texture.quote: The lesion surface appears dry and ashy with a fine peppered stippling of black microsclerotia, while the adjacent stem shows splitting and exposed pale fibrous tissue.

color.quote: Affected foreground rows along the field edge show a uniform tan to golden-brown canopy of prematurely senescent plants, contrasting sharply with the still-green plants further into the field.

texture.quote: Plants in the affected rows appear dry, wilted, and prematurely dried-down, with foliage collapsed and brittle in appearance rather than turgid and green.

color.quote: Numerous pale yellow to light green chlorotic flecks scattered across the otherwise green soybean leaflet.

shape.quote: Lesions appear as small, irregular angular flecks and blotches, many roughly star- or branch-shaped rather than perfectly round.

margin.quote: Spot edges fade gradually into surrounding green tissue with no sharp dark border or halo.

color.quote: Numerous small lesions on the upper leaf surface appear as pale tan to light grayish-brown patches set against medium-green healthy tissue, with some surrounding chlorotic yellow-green halos.

shape.quote: Lesions are small, irregularly shaped angular blotches scattered densely across the leaflets, varying in size from pinpoint flecks to coalescing patches a few millimeters across.

margin.quote: Lesion edges are diffuse and poorly defined, fading gradually into the surrounding green tissue without a sharp dark border.

value: no sporulation visible from upper surface
texture.quote: From the upper-surface view shown, the lesions appear flat to slightly sunken and dry; the characteristic downy sporulation on the lower leaf surface cannot be assessed in this image.

color.quote: The lesions show small tan-to-grayish centers ringed by a distinct reddish-brown to dark purple-brown border on a green soybean leaf.

shape.quote: Numerous small, mostly round to slightly angular spots are scattered densely across the leaf blade.

margin.quote: Each spot has a sharply delimited dark reddish-brown margin separating the pale center from the surrounding green tissue.

texture.quote: The lesion centers appear thin, dry and papery, and several have fallen out producing shot-hole perforations across the leaf.

color.quote: Lesion centers are pale tan to gray surrounded by reddish to dark purple-brown narrow rings, set against a chlorotic yellow-green leaf background with dense purple-brown speckling.

shape.quote: Spots are small, roughly round to slightly oval, distinct individual lesions scattered across the leaf rather than coalescing.

margin.value: narrow dark reddish-brown to purple border
margin.quote: Lesion margins are sharply defined with a narrow dark reddish-brown to purple ring bordering the pale tan-gray center, with no diffuse halo.

texture.quote: Lesion centers appear dry, flat, and thin-papery against the leaf surface; no visible fuzz, mold, or sporulation can be resolved in this image.

color.quote: The lower stem shows extensive dark brown to reddish-brown discoloration extending up from the base, contrasting with the still-green upper stem and petioles.

shape.quote: The lesion is an elongated, diffuse band of discoloration running longitudinally along the main stem rather than a discrete spot, extending well above the soil line.

margin.quote: The boundary between the diseased dark-brown tissue and the healthy green stem above is gradual and indistinct, fading rather than forming a sharp edge.

texture.quote: The affected stem appears dry and slightly shrunken with the characteristic soybean trichomes still present, and surrounding leaves are wilted and brown but no fungal sporulation or fruiting bodies are clearly visible on the stem surface.

color.quote: Affected rows show collapsed, tan-to-brown dead and dying plants standing out against the surrounding healthy green canopy, with some chlorotic yellow-green tissue at the transition zones.

shape.value: linear strips of stunted and dead plants running along entire crop rows, producing patchy gaps in the stand
shape.quote: Damage appears as elongated linear strips of stunted and dead plants running along entire crop rows, producing patchy gaps within the otherwise uniform soybean stand.

texture.quote: Diseased rows look wilted and collapsed with shriveled foliage, leaving a thinned, sparse canopy that exposes the bare brown soil between the rows.

color.quote: The lower stems show conspicuous white mycelial growth at the soil line transitioning into bleached tan and brown necrotic stem tissue above.

shape.value: girdling band at the stem base extending upward as elongated bleached stem lesions
shape.quote: The lesions appear as elongated girdling bands wrapping the lower stems at and just above the soil line, extending upward as bleached stem cankers.

margin.value: diffuse, feathery boundary between white mycelium and discolored stem tissue
margin.quote: The boundary between the white fungal growth and the discolored stem tissue is diffuse and feathery rather than sharply defined.

texture.quote: Dense cottony, fan-like white mycelial mats coat the base of the stems and spread onto adjacent soil and debris.

color.quote: The lower stem at/near the soil line shows a bleached tan-to-cream discoloration with a few small darker brown flecks, contrasting with the normal woody brown of the stem above.

shape.value: elongated girdling lesion along lower stem
shape.quote: The affected area forms an elongated, vertically oriented patch wrapping the lower stem just below the root mass, consistent with a crown/stem-base girdling lesion.

margin.quote: The boundary between bleached diseased tissue and the darker healthy stem is irregular and somewhat diffuse rather than sharply defined.

texture.quote: The lesion surface appears dry, papery, and bleached with a few small dark specks; no clear white mycelial mat or distinct tan sclerotia are visible in this single photo.

color.quote: Dense tan to orange-brown rust-colored pustules cover the pale green abaxial leaf surface, with a few darker brown necrotic flecks scattered among them.

shape.quote: Lesions appear as numerous small, roughly circular pinpoint pustules scattered across the leaf underside rather than large coalesced spots.

margin.quote: Individual pustules have diffuse, indistinct edges that blend into the surrounding leaf tissue without a sharp chlorotic halo.

texture.quote: The lesions present as raised granular pustules giving the leaf surface a finely powdery, dusty appearance characteristic of active rust sporulation.

color.quote: Numerous small tan to reddish-brown flecks are scattered across both leaves on a background that ranges from chlorotic yellow-green on the right leaf to an advanced uniform yellow-tan on the left leaf.

shape.quote: Lesions appear as very small, angular to polygonal flecks confined between leaf veins, densely peppered across the lamina rather than forming large discrete spots.

margin.quote: Individual lesion margins are diffuse and blur into the surrounding chlorotic tissue, with their outer edges effectively bounded by minor veins rather than by a sharp halo.

texture.quote: The leaf surfaces show a fine dusty stippled texture from coalescing flecks, though individual pustules and sporulation cannot be clearly resolved at this image scale.
